current.summary: Deep research on provenance/versioning + terminology + 70+ agent landscape mapping + authored Agent Skills Metadata Convention as companion to agentskills.io + 2 rounds of 5-persona review

activeWork.description: Agent Skills Metadata Convention — companion convention to agentskills.io adding provenance, triggers, expectations, tags, status, supported_agents
activeWork.progress: Convention spec is drafted and reviewed twice by 5 personas. Must-fixes applied (RFC 2119, derived_from as array, content_hash algorithm, SKILL.md chicken-and-egg). Should-fixes applied (convention URI semantics, mode interaction matrix, license precedence, forward compat). Tags and status fields added as Draft. Discussion doc written. Remaining: discussion doc improvements (source 650-trial study, concrete graduation thresholds, signing roadmap, concrete next steps).

recentDecisions: Terminology: 'agent' is the public-facing term everywhere; 'harness' is internal syllago docs only; 'provider' renamed to 'model provider' for LLM vendors
Spec strategy: companion convention using agentskills.io's metadata extension point (OTel + Schema.org hybrid pattern), NOT a fork or replacement
Stability levels: Provenance = Stable, Triggers/Expectations/Tags/Status/Supported Agents = Draft
derived_from is always an array (no object/array polymorphism)
content_hash hashes raw bytes (no line ending normalization), SKILL.md included with hash value emptied
convention URI is an opaque identifier, exact string comparison, agents MUST NOT dereference
Graduation path: Draft → Stable → Proposed for Core → Agent Skills Spec, based on demonstrated adoption
nextSteps: Apply discussion doc improvements: source 650-trial study link, propose '3 agents + 2 registries' graduation threshold, add signing roadmap ('v0.2 will define signatures'), state content_hash will become REQUIRED for registry-distributed content, add concrete next steps with dates
Update provenance research doc (2026-03-30-provenance-versioning-research.md) to use 'agent' terminology instead of 'provider'
Consider separate session: drill into content type support matrix data (format convergence, correlation analysis, gap analysis for syllago — use the prompt generated for this)
Begin syllago implementation: rename 'provider' to 'agent' across public-facing surfaces (CLI flags, TUI labels, docs)
Implement metadata convention parsing in syllago as reference implementation
Present to Agent Ecosystem community with concrete deliverables

context.notes: This session was massive — ~8 hours of deep research and spec work. Key research docs created: provenance-versioning-research.md (20+ specs analyzed), provider-agent-naming.md (70+ agents mapped with content type support matrix). The convention was reviewed twice by 5 simulated personas (skill author, agent developer, registry maintainer, enterprise security, spec pedant). V2 moved from 'NOT READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION' to 'almost implementable with 4 small fixes' — those fixes are now applied. A prompt for a separate data analysis session was generated and given to Holden (format convergence, content type correlation, gap analysis). The old handoff 'pause-skill-md-spec-implementation-to-align' was the predecessor — that session identified the terminology problem, this session resolved it and went far beyond.

previousSessions.summary: Pause skill.md spec implementation to align terminology (platforms vs providers vs harnesses) across specs before writing fields
completedWork: Identified terminology inconsistency blocking spec work: agent ecosystem says 'platforms', syllago says 'providers', others say 'harnesses'
nextSteps: Research terminology usage across agent ecosystem spec, syllago, and other tools
Update skill.md spec with aligned vocabulary
